module.name: common
module.purpose: Shared common crate root for reusable config loading with default CoreConfig fallback, server IP and handshake token resolution from plain config values, external re-export adapters, database telemetry logging, and structure utilities. It exposes WorkQueue cleanup support used by server host-disconnect session reset, and lib.rs re-exports serializable config system types Arch, Platform, and System alongside Config, ServerConfig, and related models, plus the Logger telemetry client with LogMessage, LogTag, and LoggingError.

childModules.name: config
childModules.purpose: Owns the common crate configuration model, config-file discovery, serializable OS/architecture System detection, the model API key, server IP, and server handshake auth token as plain config values, a replaceable config cache with Config::load and test-only Config::mock overrides, runtime path normalization, default CoreConfig fallback, client settings without endpoint addresses, server runtime IP, handshake token, and port settings, and tool directory settings.

childModules.name: external
childModules.purpose: Owns public re-export wrappers for third-party libraries so downstream crates can route third-party access through common where appropriate, including the redb embedded-database wrapper backing telemetry storage.

childModules.name: logging
childModules.purpose: Owns the database telemetry logging system: the serializable LogTag and LogMessage records, the LoggingError enum, and the Logger client that hosts a telemetry server accepting authenticated remoc channel connections and persisting messages to a fresh per-start timestamped local redb database, or connects to a remote server and streams LogMessage records over the authenticated channel transport.

childModules.name: structure
childModules.purpose: Owns shared structural data interfaces such as WorkQueue, whose clear() method empties working and completed entries for session cleanup, plus channel helpers whose accept and connect entry points select a client, host, or telemetry endpoint and resolve its address and token from config before running a shared-secret token handshake.
